statement.focus_question: Or is this true only for almost all $n$, and $\limsup f(n)/n=\infty$?
statement.normalized: Let $f(n)$ be the minimal integer $m$ such that $n$ is the sum of the $k$ smallest divisors of $m$ for some $k\geq 1$. Is it true that $f(n)=o(n)$? Or is this true only for almost all $n$, and $\limsup f(n)/n=\infty$?
statement.original:
Let $f(n)$ be the minimal integer $m$ such that $n$ is the sum of the $k$ smallest divisors of $m$ for some $k\geq 1$.

Is it true that $f(n)=o(n)$? Or is this true only for almost all $n$, and $\limsup f(n)/n=\infty$?
